reviews.review_description: Ok, seems like decent endless runner/shooter Game, fun enough for free, might even be worth say 99 cents to upgrade. When you simply lose your "repairs" in the game, it asks if you want to "repair" your ship, to simply play on, in this little game, for either 99 cents or 1.49 USD to simply continue playing.  WTF are the thinking? Does the developer really think that people are that stupid? Could be that stupid?  Not me, the utter audacity of such a profit model, is simply stunning, I dunno maybe next will ask for 50 dollars? Ridiculous,  uninstalled, done

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. This game doesn't have to cost money, you keep accumulating energy  and repair with energy. If you don't have an energy, just restart.

reviews.review_description: The game is free but you can buy upgrades and continues. I've spent about $4 and that's enough for me to unlock all 4 ships as well as have the 3rd highest score on the leader board. Super fun and it reminds me of the classic game Descent.

A really fun game that too for free. And as mentioned in previous review, I guess he misunderstood the concept of free games. You dont have to buy gems if you don't want to (it's not necessary).. and you can continue playing game and earn gems while playing. I liked this game and it's absolutely free and worth downloading. Cheers to developer for making it free.
My space craft can just shoot but I have no control on speed or it heights.  What should I do? Are there controls guidelines?

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry we didn't add this guideline. When your head rotating, your craft will move to the direction of your head.

reviews.review_description: I have a feeling I’ll be in the distinct minority in giving this game the thumbs down, especially since it’s free, but I would never lie in a review. At first, I would have sworn this title was developed and released back in 2015-2016, which actually would have provided it some goodwill. It’s not just that the graphics look cheap and low-quality, it’s that compared to other titles of this same genre, it is analogous to playing a PS4 then going back to Super Nintendo. For me, the game lacked any kind of immersion or thrills, the creatures looked lame and cheesy, and the whole Saturday-morning-cartoon art design just didn’t work for me. I know, I know, I must be super fun at parties. I didn’t hate it, and I don’t begrudge anyone who disagrees with me. I just feel like compared to, say Voxel Fly, this comes up woefully short. That it’s a sequel and only 8 months old is even more perplexing. The spaceship in this game won’t have anything on you if you were to just dodge this game altogether.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, Space Doge 2 will stay updated. In future updates, art and game mechanics will be enriched.

reviews.review_description: You do not need to use in app purchases in order to play this game. You earn gems & repairs during gameplay. The only reason you'd make an in app purchase would be to support the developers, which would be highly recommended or you're impatient & choose to not earn gems & repairs through gameplay.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your support, Space Doge 2 will continue to be updated.
